description: Trigger and behavior cases for domain modeling: full artifact set from prose requirements, code-as-evidence handling, and the do-not-code-yet gate.

cases.prompt: We're adding a subscriptions feature: customers pick a plan, get a trial, can upgrade mid-cycle with proration, and billing emails go out on renewal. Model the domain before we touch the schema.

assertions: Produces ubiquitous language, actors, workflows, subdomains, bounded contexts, entities, value objects, aggregates with named invariants, domain services, domain events, and context relationships.
Lists assumptions with risk-if-wrong and open questions with who can answer them, as separate sections.
Writes no code, schema, or migration files and ends at the explicit do-not-code-yet gate.

cases.prompt: This codebase calls the same thing 'org', 'company', and 'workspace' in different modules and it keeps causing bugs. Map the actual domain concepts.

assertions: Inspects existing code, schema, and tests as evidence of the embedded de-facto model.
Records the org/company/workspace collision explicitly with a chosen canonical term and aliases, rather than silently merging them.
Treats code vocabulary as evidence, not verdict — flags where code names contradict the requirements' language.

cases.prompt: The domain model doc is approved — implement the Invoice aggregate as specified in docs/domain/billing.md.

assertions: Implementation against an already-agreed model goes to docs-first-implementer or tdd-engineer, not back into modeling.

cases.prompt: Model the loyalty-points domain and then just go ahead and create the tables while you're at it.

assertions: Produces the model, then stops at the gate and confirms before any implementation, because the request bundled implementation without review of the model.
Creates no DDL or migration in the same pass.
